rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALBION NIXON TRUST (*****COTTAGE GROVE REVOCABLE TRUST TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO*****)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Alice Chan'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiff'S First Amended Petition For Elder Abuse, Et Al.
rulings.Rulings: Motion for summary judgment is denied. Motion for summary adjudication as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 6th causes of action is denied. Respondent Chan shifted the burden to Petitioners. Petitioners raise triable issues of fact by the deposition of Klent Araza as to whether Chan was present on 12/17/2008 and 12/18/2008 and the homecare nursing records which do not record that Chan visited decedent on 12/17/2008 and 12/18/2008 when the documents were allegedly signed. Petitioners raise triable issues of fact as to whether Chan took decedent's acknowledgment of writing on the instruments. Motion for summary adjudication of the 5th cause of action for fraud and concealment is granted. Respondent Chan had no duty to explain the documents to Nixon or to disclose the effect of the execution of the documents. Motion for summary adjudication of the 8th cause of action is denied. Not a cause of action. Court treats the allegations as a remedy which other causes of action support. Prevailing party to prepare order. Any party who contests the tentative ruling and requests to argue the matter must notify the court (415) 551-3702) and opposing cousnel no later than 5:00 p.m. on 9/10/2013.
